title: Keir Starmer’s last stand, at the Nato summit
gist: Andrew Adonis argues that Britain's rock-solid, ~£3bn/year support for Ukraine amid post-Brexit Europhobia exposes Brexit as only ever a half-withdrawal from Europe, and predicts UK leaders' Ukraine leadership may pave the way to EU re-entry.

insights.claim: Ten years after the Brexit referendum, British support for Ukraine has been rock solid despite Europe being 'the curse of our politics'—Europhobia dissolves at the border with Ukraine.
insights.speaker: Andrew Adonis
insights.so_what: Signals durable UK defence commitment to Ukraine regardless of domestic anti-EU politics.

insights.claim: UK military support for Ukraine has run at some £3bn or more every year since the 2022 Russian invasion, with virtually no political opposition, not even from Nigel Farage despite Trump's praise for Putin.
insights.speaker: Andrew Adonis
insights.so_what: Cross-party consensus makes UK Ukraine funding a low political-risk line item for defence exposure.

insights.claim: In barely a year Europe has replaced the entirety of the US military aid to Ukraine that Trump withdrew after taking office in January 2025, contrary to Trump's expectation the Europeans would also abandon Ukraine.
insights.speaker: Andrew Adonis
insights.so_what: European burden-shift is a structural shift in defence-spending demand across the continent.
insights.locator: Appropriately, Keir Starmer’s last major act

insights.claim: Defence was deliberately excluded from Brexit—no leading Brexiter proposed leaving NATO, and they used continued NATO membership as proof Brexit was 'safe'.
insights.speaker: Andrew Adonis
insights.so_what: Explains why UK security integration with Europe survived Brexit intact.

insights.claim: Brexiters are now among those most vocal about avoiding a 'Trump-exit' from NATO, which would threaten UK and European security.
insights.speaker: Andrew Adonis
insights.so_what: Highlights the incoherence in the Eurosceptic position on European security.

insights.claim: Brexit was only ever a half-withdrawal from Europe's collective institutions, and the Ukraine war both demonstrated this and gave Britain a chance to lead the NATO-enshrined defence component of the pan-European fabric.
insights.speaker: Andrew Adonis
insights.so_what: Frames UK-EU security convergence as an ongoing, not settled, question.
insights.locator: In reality, Brexit was only ever

insights.claim: Adonis suspects that Boris Johnson, Rishi Sunak, Liz Truss and Keir Starmer rising to the Ukraine challenge may have paved the way for Britain's re-entry to the EU itself in due course.
insights.speaker: Andrew Adonis
insights.so_what: A long-horizon signal that UK-EU rejoin is being seeded by defence cooperation.

insights.claim: Short-lived, failing British governments have become the norm in the decade since Brexit, with Starmer framed as an outgoing prime minister whose last major act is the Ankara NATO summit.
insights.speaker: Andrew Adonis
insights.so_what: Political instability premium remains a backdrop for UK asset risk.
